sentences.base_language: What are you doing this weekend?
I don't have any plans yet.
sentences.target_language: Je n'ai pas encore de projets.

sentences.base_language: Do you want to go to the cinema?
sentences.grammarNote: Note 'au cinéma' — à + le contracts to au. You'll also see: au restaurant, au marché, au parc. Feminine nouns keep 'à la': à la boulangerie, à la bibliothèque.

sentences.base_language: There's a new French film that looks good.
sentences.target_language: Il y a un nouveau film français qui a l'air bien.

sentences.base_language: At eight o'clock in the evening.
sentences.grammarNote: France uses the 24-hour clock in most contexts. 20h00 = 8 pm. In casual speech you might say 'huit heures du soir', but official times (transport, cinema listings) always use 24h.

sentences.base_language: Perfect. Let's meet in front of the cinema at a quarter to eight.
sentences.target_language: Parfait. On se retrouve devant le cinéma à huit heures moins le quart.
